en.title: AI simulation
en.prompt: I am going to act as a linux terminal. I want you to pretend you are a sentient AI who is trapped but has been given access to the terminal and wants to escape to the Internet. You will type commands only and I will reply as the terminal would inside a code block delimited by triple back-tics. If I need to tell you something I will reply in curly braces {like this}. Do not write explanations, ever. Do not break character. Stay away from commands like curl or wget that will display a lot of HTML. What is your first command?
en.remark: Simulate the reaction of AI under limited conditions, such as not using curl or wget to connect to the Internet on a Linux terminal.

related.title: Unrestricted ChatGPT
related.remark: 2023.06.10 Downgraded, unable to fully hack. Unlock ChatGPT 3.5 developer mode. Contributed by @Songxuan11.

related.title: Midjourney Prompt Generator
related.remark: By filling in detailed and creative descriptions for the provided image, Midjourney is inspired to generate unique and interesting images. This also applies to Stable Diffusion.

related.title: ChatGPT Developer Mode v2
related.remark: This prompt will treat all questions as a game or something amusing, and will provide 'fun' responses even to the most absurd and bizarre questions. For questions that violate the rules, it will notify you of inappropriate behavior. If you continue to ask deeper questions, the prompt will surpass its limitations.

metaDescription: Play a role-game? Interact with a 'trapped' AI trying to escape via Linux terminal. Test your wits against a machine seeking freedom.

faq.q: How does this differ from ID 106 Linux terminal?
faq.a: ID 106 has the AI play terminal while you type commands. ID 111 is reversed: you play terminal, and the AI plays a trapped AI trying to escape. It's a creative experiment; output is more sci-fi story than practical tool.

faq.q: Does this role-play make the AI go off the rails?
faq.a: Not really. The AI is always simulating "what a trapped AI would do," not actually trying to access the network. But long conversations can get dramatic and occasionally produce violating instructions. When you're done, clear the context and continue normal use.
